rulings.Case Title: YING-TSUN SU VS. NATASHA V. ALEXEEVA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint And Amended Answer To Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANTS PAUL HWANG AND SKYBOX REALTY, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AND AMENDED ANSWER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted in full. Responses to the first amended cross-complaint must be filed within ten calendar days of a filed order granting this motion. California law and policy liberally allows the filing of amended pleadings. The claims sought to be added to the cross-complaint are based on the same core set of alleged facts and disputes about those alleged facts that are already alleged in the parties' pleadings. Neither Ms. Alexeeva nor Mr. Su has shown that they will suffer legal prejudice by the granting of this motion or that the Skybox parties have acted in bad faith in filing this motion. No argument has been made as to the portion of the motion seeking to amend the answer of the Skybox parties. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENTH AND MARKET, LLC VS. JOHN MEAD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Deft John Mead
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF TENTH AND MARKET, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT JOHN MEAD is denied. Surpassing the unaddressed procedural issues that leave of court was neither sought nor given for plaintiff to file a first amended complaint (FAC) based on a 3-day notice that was served after this action was filed (see CCP 464) and defendant John Mead arguably waived that irregularity by filing an answer to the FAC, as a matter of law the FAC, the predicate for this motion, is insufficient to support any unlawful detainer relief. Paragraph 16 of the FAC alleges that Mr. Mead's tenancy is subject to the San Francisco Rent Control Ordinance (SFRCO). As revealed in paragraph 11 of the FAC and the Three-Day Notice to Cure or Quit dated January 22, 2024 referred to and appended to the FAC, plaintiff's sole alleged reason for seeking possession of the premises is based on the alleged expiration of a fixed-term residential lease. Section 37.9(a) of the SFRCO permits recovery of possession of residential premises based on one or more of sixteen specified "just cause" reasons for eviction. Expiration of a fixed-term residential lease is not one of those sixteen reasons. (Compare SFRCO 37.9(a)(5)). Accordingly, plaintiff has failed to sustain its summary motion burden to show entitlement to judgment in its favor. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VALLEJO POWELL SRO, LLC VS. JUN PING DU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT JUN DU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with five days leave to file a second amended complaint if it can do so in good faith in conformity with this order. The FAC fails to adequately allege two essential facts for a non-payment of rent eviction: 1) the existence of a landlord-tenant relationship and 2) notice to defendant of the last day of the three-day notice period. Plaintiff may correct the first failure by completely filling out paragraph 6(b) of the form complaint. Plaintiff may correct the second failure by alleging facts that defendant was either explicitly informed of the last day of the three-day notice period or that sufficient information was provided to defendant to enable the defendant to determine that date by identifying the facts that were provided to defendant to make that determination. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: REARDON HEIGHTS ASSOCIATES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA VS. KIERRA I. JAMES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT KIERRA JAMES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The CARES Act requires a 30 day cure period. Because the notice served on defendant did not provide for a 30 day cure period, the notice is deficient as a matter of law and there is no reasonable possibility that plaintiff can amend to correct this deficiency. While no published California case has addressed the issue raised by this demurrer, Sherwood v. Pinzon (2022) Wash. App. 664 is an on-point well-reasoned decision that is likely to be followed by the appellate courts of this state. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISE, INC. VS. DANIEL LEE & CO., INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT DANIEL LEE & CO., INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. The asserted failure of a landlord to serve a three-day notice in the manner contemplated by the lease does not affect this Court's subject matter or personal jurisdiction and thus may not be raised by a motion to quash. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
